title: Watch: How Mollywood Times defies the Myth of the Roadmap | Sapne vs Everyone | FMM 24
description: Everybody tells us to follow our dreams. But what happens when nobody can agree on the roadmap? In this episode of Frequently Made Mistakes, Sudhish Kamath explores a common screenwriting problem in dream-chasing stories: writers creating the illusion of uncertainty while secretly guiding their protagonists through a predetermined path.Using Mollywood Times and Sapne vs Everyone Season 2 as case studies, we examine why these stories feel more honest, contemporary and emotionally resonant than many conventional success narratives.If you’re a screenwriter, filmmaker, storyteller or simply someone fascinated by stories about chasing impossible dreams, this episode is for you.

Her flight, a political statement by Nikita Khrushchev, lasted nearly three days, completing 48 orbits. Tereshkova's achievement, despite lacking formal pilot or engineering training, was celebrated globally, though she never flew again.

This procurement aims to bolster preparedness against a wide array of modern aerial adversaries.

title: Second wife denied family pension even after first wife’s death; what court said
description: A Punjab woman, the second wife of a deceased government employee, has won a legal battle for her full family pension. The High Court ruled she is entitled to 100% of the pension, rejecting the state's claim to cap it at 50%. The court found the state's interpretation of rules was incorrect, as she was the sole surviving widow.

title: Why India's 'Tomahawk' could be as important as BrahMos in future conflicts
description: Brahmos missiles swiftly neutralized Pakistan's air power on May 10, 2025, demonstrating decisive capability. However, prolonged modern conflicts necessitate a cost-effective solution. India is exploring the Long Range Land Attack Cruise Missile (LR-LACM), a cheaper, 1,500 km range weapon, to complement Brahmos and enhance sustained combat operations, ensuring greater strategic staying power.

His goal celebration, a hand gesture resembling a gun, drew mixed reactions amid pre-match political protests. title: Anurag Kashyap slams theatres for 'killing' films like 'Bandar' and 'Main Vaapas Aaunga'

Kashyap revealed he gets upset when fans praise his films years later on OTT, noting they never supported them in theatres. The filmmaker warns that prioritizing massive event movies like 'Dhurandhar' over word-of-mouth films is a dangerous financial strategy for cinema halls.

The woman alleges her former acquaintance, Ayyaz Taj Madare, subjected her to rituals, spiked her drink, and extorted money before forcing a conversion and marriage.

title: Karan Johar backs 'Odiyan'; Prithviraj, Manju Warrier lead folklore mystery

Directed by Rahul Sadasivan, the film blends folklore and mystery, promising a unique pan-Indian release across five languages. The amendment to the Drugs Rules, 1945, was finalized after considering public and stakeholder feedback.
